title: lobelia trullifolia subsp. minor
intro: Lobelia trullifolia subsp. minor, also known as small-leaf lobelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, characterized by its small leaves and blue or purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Lobelia trullifolia subsp. minor include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and acting as a natural expectorant to aid in the removal of mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions.
